<!-- Generated by the upstream-render-rebase-analysis workflow, 2026-06-01. -->

# Upstream render replacement — what changed & overlap with our work

## 0. TL;DR

Upstream's 3 commits (`origin/main` @ `9ff7a69`, 3 ahead of merge-base `3a384ec`) **replace the entire classic software renderer** with a faithful OpenRSC port under `render/orsc/`. They **delete** `render/{boundary,camera,facesort,model,palette,pick,rasterize,roof,scene,scenery,surface,terrain,textures,textures_test}.go` and **gut** `render/render.go` (574→32 lines) down to the `View`/`Entity` value types plus sprite-compositing shims (`composite_export.go`). The render entry point is now `orsc.RenderView` / `orsc.RenderViewCached` (live, host-centred) and `orsc.RenderBridge` (the colleague's render-diff hook).

Net effect on our render work:

- **5 of our 7 fidelity fixes (`276b58a`) are already correct in orsc.** orsc is a from-scratch faithful port of `World.java`, so it never had the bugs we patched in the old renderer. Those patches are **mooted** — their target files no longer exist.
- **2 of our fixes are NOT in orsc and must be re-ported**: the **diagonal-door / diagonal-object pass (48001..59999)** (`a8ab7d4`) and the **id-74 windmill-sail vertical lift (−480)** (`276b58a`). orsc has genuine gaps here, identical to the bugs we found.
- **Our render-diff tooling is COMPLEMENTARY, not duplicative.** orsc's `harness.go`/`composite_export.go` are a single-engine PNG render hook; they have no serializable dump format, no structural face-set diff, no pixel-diff CLI, and no JAR-oracle leg. Our `rscdump/1` schema + `cmd/renderdiff` + the rscplus JAR oracle supply all four. But our `dump.go`/`dump_faces.go`/`diagobj.go` import the now-deleted classic `render` API and **must be retargeted onto `orsc`**.
- **The deob (Jagex-jar lineage) remains a fully independent oracle.** orsc is OpenRSC lineage; the deob/jar is Jagex rev-235 lineage — two different codebases of the same game. Diffing orsc against the deob/jar is exactly the cross-lineage faithfulness check. The DEOB render audit stays relevant.

---

## 1. The `render/orsc` renderer

By its own `doc.go`, `render/orsc` is a **line-for-line Go port of OpenRSC's `three/` software renderer** (`RSModel.java`, `Scene.java`, `Shader.java`, `World.java`, `Polygon.java`, `Scanline.java`). It is deliberately the **second independent renderer** in the repo, so the two engines can render the same scene and be diffed pixel-for-pixel.

### Public entry points (the old `RenderView`/`Scene`/`Surface`/`GameModel` equivalents)

| Old `render/` concept | orsc equivalent | File |
|---|---|---|
| `RenderView(land,f,…) → PNG` (the ~574-line body removed from render.go) | `orsc.RenderView(land, f, models, sprites, v render.View) ([]byte, error)`; cached `orsc.RenderViewCached(land, f, v)` | `render/orsc/view.go` |
| `Scene` (model list, camera, rasterizer) | `orsc.Scene` via `NewScene(surf, maxModels, maxPolygons, maxSpriteFaces)`; `AddModel`, `AddEntity`, `SetCamera`, `Render` | `render/orsc/scene.go`, `types.go` |
| `Surface` (framebuffer) | `orsc.Surface`, `NewSurface(w,h)`, `toImage()`; `Surface.Pixels []int32` = Java `GraphicsController.pixelData` | `render/orsc/surface.go`, `doc.go` |
| `GameModel` (geometry accumulator) | `orsc.Model` / `RSModel`: `NewModel`, `FromAssets`, `insertVertex`, `insertFace`, `Orient`, `Translate` | `render/orsc/model.go`, `world.go` |
| `buildTerrain` / `BuildBoundaries` / `BuildRoofs` / `PlaceScenery` | `orsc.BuildTerrain` (world.go), `orsc.BuildWalls`/`wallPass`/`doorPass` (walls.go), `orsc.BuildRoofs`/`roofPass` (roofs.go), `orsc.BuildStories` (stories.go), `placeSceneryModels`+`PlaceScenery` (scenery_place.go, world.go) | as listed |

### `render/render.go` is gone, not a shim

`render.go` no longer renders anything. It is reduced to the `View` struct (camera params + live dynamic-state hooks: `BoundaryRemoved`, `DynamicScenery`, `SceneryRemoved`, `GroundItems`, self-appearance), `DynamicSceneryItem`, `GroundItemMarker`, and entity/item-sprite compositing. The `RenderView` **function** relocated wholesale into `orsc/view.go`; `cmd/cradle` now calls `orsc.RenderViewCached(land, f, v)` (`cmd/cradle/main.go`, `cmd/cradle/spectate.go`). `render/` is now a pure data/compositing helper package that orsc imports.

### The colleague's diff/export infra

- **`render/orsc/harness.go`** — `RenderBridge(x,y,plane,rot,zoom,w,h) ([]byte,error)`: opens the `.orsc` assets, builds terrain/scenery/stories/entities, drives the spectator camera, returns PNG bytes. It is a **render-to-PNG hook for a manual two-engine pixel diff** — there is no diff code, no serialization, no face-set comparison. (It still hardcodes `/Users/flint/Code/openrsc` asset paths.)
- **`render/composite_export.go`** — exported wrappers (`CompositePlayerSprite`, `CompositeNPCSprite`, `NPCBillboardSize`, `CompositePlayerAppearanceSprite`) so orsc reuses `render/`'s verified entity compositing. Pure code reuse, not diff infra.

A grep of `render/orsc/*.go` for `json|Dump|Serialize|BuiltFace|rscdump|PixelDiff|Marshal` returns **zero code hits**. orsc has **no serializable cross-engine dump format and no structural diff** — only the in-process PNG hook.

---

## 2. Bug-overlap table

Legend: **ALREADY-FIXED-UPSTREAM** = orsc's faithful port already does the correct thing (our patch is mooted, target file deleted); **PORT-FORWARD** = orsc has the same gap, must re-implement; **MOOT/SUPERSEDED** = no port needed.

| Bug | Our fix (file) | orsc status + cite | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Diagonal scenery objects / diagonal doors (48001..59999)** | `render/diagobj.go` `BuildDiagonalObjects` — ports `World.addModels` (`a8ab7d4`) | **NOT HANDLED.** orsc handles diagonal **walls** only in `1..11999` and `12001..23999` (`walls.go`, `roofs.go`). The `48001..59999` object band is never read for geometry; scenery is placed exclusively from `f.SceneryLocs` (`scenery_place.go`), not from inline 48000+ landscape markers. `world.go` documents the band but no pass consumes it. Grep for `addModels`/`48000`/`getTileDirection` in orsc = **0 code hits**. | **PORT FORWARD (HIGH VALUE).** A diagonal door renders as **zero geometry** in orsc — exactly the "door the cradle can't perceive" bug. |
| **Water-flatten by `tileType==4`** (not overlay id 2/11) | `render/terrain.go` (`276b58a`) | **ALREADY CORRECT.** orsc flattens a corner to y=0 iff any touching tile has `typeOf(id)==4` (`world.go`); overlay 2/11 are type 3 and not flattened. | **ALREADY-FIXED-UPSTREAM / MOOT** (terrain.go deleted). |
| **Type-2 indoor floor-split gate** | `render/terrain.go` (`276b58a`) | **ALREADY CORRECT.** orsc gates the seam split on tile-type class via `isTileType2` and the `decorType != 2 \|\| diagonal` branch (`world.go`). | **ALREADY-FIXED-UPSTREAM / MOOT.** |
| **Wall front/back colour via `method422`** (not stone/wood heuristic) | `render/boundary.go` + `dump.go` (`276b58a`) | **ALREADY CORRECT.** orsc's `insertWall` passes `def.FrontDeco`/`def.BackDeco` verbatim into `insertFace(...)` (`walls.go`) — method422's per-face fill, no material heuristic. | **ALREADY-FIXED-UPSTREAM / MOOT.** |
| **id-74 vertical lift (−480)** (windmill sails atop tower) | `render/scenery.go` (`276b58a`) | **NOT HANDLED.** orsc places scenery via `PlaceScenery`→`SceneryCentre` with `setRot256(0,dir*32,0)` + ground-anchor translate and **no per-id Y offset**. No `74`/`480`/`lift` token in scenery_place.go/world.go/stories.go. | **PORT FORWARD (cosmetic).** orsc renders the sails splayed at terrain level. |
| **Terrain split by tile-type class** (not raw overlay id) | `render/terrain.go` (`276b58a`) | **ALREADY CORRECT.** Seam-revert compares `isTileType2(x±1,z)` vs `decorType2` by class (`world.go`). | **ALREADY-FIXED-UPSTREAM / MOOT.** |
| **Overlay neighbour-spread** (type-4 quad onto adjacent non-type-3 tiles) | `render/terrain.go` (`276b58a`) | **ALREADY CORRECT.** orsc's deck/overhang second pass (`emitDeckQuad`, `world.go`) emits a deck quad onto each non-type-3 tile bordering a type-4 tile — the `World.java` overhang. | **ALREADY-FIXED-UPSTREAM / MOOT.** |
| **Roof under-cull set from `overlayDef.tileType==2`** | `render/roof.go` (`276b58a`) | **N/A — DESIGN SUPERSEDED.** orsc builds roofs as real 3D geometry (`roofPass`) resolved by the painter's-algorithm plane-overlap sort. The cull set was an artifact of the old flat-overlay renderer. | **MOOT / no port.** Verify roofs occlude interiors visually; nothing to port. |

**Summary:** 5 fidelity fixes are baked into orsc and vanish cleanly on rebase (target files deleted, no textual conflict). **2 real gaps to port forward:** diagonal-door objects (high value — the cradle-perceptibility bug) and id-74 sail lift (cosmetic).

---

## 3. Tooling-overlap verdict: COMPLEMENTARY → RE-PORT-OURS-ONTO-ORSC

### What each side has

| Capability | orsc `harness`/`composite_export` | Our `rscdump` + `cmd/renderdiff` + JAR oracle |
|---|---|---|
| Render a scene to PNG for visual compare | ✅ `RenderBridge` (one engine, in-process) | ✅ `RenderDump` |
| Serializable cross-engine state format (one blob, 3 engines load) | ❌ none | ✅ `rscdump/1` schema (`internal/rscdump/schema.go`) — `SourceGo`/`SourceDeob`/`SourceJarReplay` |
| Structural face-set diff ("is this door quad present?") | ❌ none | ✅ `render.RenderDumpFaces` → `[]BuiltFace` keyed by `(centroid, numVerts, fill)` (`render/dump_faces.go`); `cmd/renderdiff` set-diff |
| Pixel-diff CLI (heatmap, %, bbox, CI exit code) | ❌ (no diff code, just PNG bytes) | ✅ `cmd/renderdiff` `PixelDiff` (`cmd/renderdiff/diff.go`, `main.go`) |
| JAR-oracle leg (rev-235 obf jar renders the same fixture headless) | ❌ none | ✅ (rscplus repo, separate, documented working) |

**Verdict: our infra is strictly additive.** orsc's harness only renders one engine to PNG; it cannot diff, serialize a portable fixture, compare face-sets, or run a jar leg. The colleague explicitly *wants* a two-engine diff ("render … twice and diff the two") but only built the render half — **our `renderdiff` is the missing diff half.** Recommendation: **RE-PORT-OURS-ONTO-ORSC** (merge-both: keep our diff/dump/jar infra, retarget only the Go-engine leg from the deleted `render` API to `orsc`).

### Why a re-port is mandatory (not optional)

Our tooling **will not compile after the rebase** because it imports the deleted classic API:

- `render/dump.go`: `RenderView`, `renderViewToSurface`, `BuildBoundaries`, `Surface`/`.Pix`, `Bundle`, `wallColourWood`, `withDumpTerrainSeed`.
- `render/dump_faces.go`: `Scene`, `GameModel`, `Bundle`.
- `render/diagobj.go`: `PlaceScenery`, `GameModel`, `NewGameModel`, `AddVertex`/`AddFace`/`SetLight`, `ModelCache`, `elevationAt`, `terrainSize`, `wallColourWood`.

There is **no textual git-conflict** on these — upstream *deleted* the files our `boundary.go`/`terrain.go`/`roof.go`/`scenery.go` edits modified. The breakage is a semantic/compile break: our new files reference symbols that vanished.

### Re-port sketch (keep schema, CLI, jar leg verbatim; retarget the Go leg to orsc)

1. **Keep unchanged** (engine-agnostic): `internal/rscdump/schema.go`, `internal/rscdump/landscape.go`, `cmd/renderdiff/{main,diff}.go`, the `BuiltFace` type + `Key()`, the rscplus JAR-oracle leg. These never touched classic render internals.
2. **`render/dump.go` → retarget to orsc.** Replace `renderViewToSurface(land,f,b,v)` with `orsc.RenderView(land, f, models, sprites, v)` (or a new `orsc.RenderDump(d)` that builds `render.View` from the dump and calls `RenderView`). orsc already exposes `Surface.Pixels []int32` (framebuffer for the pixel diff) and `toImage()` (PNG). The `withDumpTerrainSeed` determinism hook must move to orsc's `terrainAmbience` (`world.go` — currently a fixed hash); add a seed override so seed-0 → flat-zero speckle for byte-identical diffs. `Bundle`/`wallColourWood`/`syntheticFacts` need orsc equivalents (orsc opens archives directly — pass `*assets.Archive`/`*assets.SpriteArchive`).
3. **`render/dump_faces.go` → retarget to orsc.** The `[]BuiltFace` export must walk `orsc.Scene.Models` (`orsc.Model` faces) instead of `render.Scene`/`GameModel`; compute the transformed-space centroid from orsc's baked `vertXTransform/Y/Z` post-`resetTransformCache`. Most involved part — needs a small `orsc` accessor to enumerate built faces + transformed centroids + `FillFront/FillBack`.
4. **`render/diagobj.go` → re-port as orsc's missing `World.addModels` pass.** Both the §2 bug fix AND a tooling dependency. Scan the window's `DiagonalWalls` for `48000 < d < 60000`, `objectID = d-48001`, `dir = tile.TileDirection`; place via orsc's `PlaceScenery`/`SceneryCentre` with the dir-0/4 footprint swap + footprint-clear for multi-tile objects. orsc's `PlaceScenery` already does footprint-centre + `dir*32` orient + bilinear ground-snap, so the body is a loop + synthetic-door fallback. Wire into `orsc.RenderView`/`RenderBridge` alongside `placeSceneryModels`. **Prerequisite:** confirm orsc's landscape read exposes `TileDirection` (upstream left `pathfind/` untouched, so `pathfind.Tile` should still carry it).
5. **Port the id-74 lift** into orsc's `PlaceScenery`/`placeSceneryModels`: `if loc.DefID == 74 { cy -= 480 }` after `SceneryCentre`.

---

## 4. Is the DEOB render audit + behavioral-bug work still relevant? YES — independent oracle

orsc is an **OpenRSC-lineage** port (`World.java`/`Scene.java`/`Shader.java` from the OpenRSC `Client_Base` tree). The deob is a **Jagex rev-235-lineage** decompile (`reference/rsc-client/`, `rsclassic-1091943135.jar`), and the rscplus JAR oracle renders that same Jagex jar. These are two **different codebases of the same game**, not two copies of one — mutually-checking oracles:

- A bug present in **both** orsc and our old renderer (e.g. the missing diagonal-object pass) is a true gap relative to the game — cross-lineage agreement confirms it.
- A divergence **between** orsc and the deob/jar localizes a faithfulness bug to one lineage. `RENDER_DEOB_GAPS.md` already catalogs **10 behavioral divergences** in the deob's own render path (texture U/V transpose, `(B,R,G)` vs `(R,G,B)` ground-ramp order, the World↔Scene alias inversion, `loadMapData` wrong-class call, etc.). Several — e.g. the ground-ramp channel order — diffed against orsc's `genColorToResource` (`world.go`) would immediately surface as a per-pixel colour shift in `renderdiff`. So the deob audit **feeds** the diff harness rather than being replaced by it.

**Conclusion:** keep the deob render audit and behavioral-bug work. The deob/jar is the **independent cross-lineage oracle** the (now-orsc) Go engine should be diffed against — precisely the role our `rscdump`+`renderdiff`+JAR-oracle infra fills, and which orsc's single-engine `harness` cannot.
